@graph.name: Human Microbiome Project
@graph.description: The Human Microbiome Project (HMP) was a Common Fund program that produced analysis data, tools, and protocols for the comprehensive characterization of the human microbiome and its role in human health and disease. It was carried out in two phases, Human Microbiome Project 1 (HMP1) and Integrative Human Microbiome Project (iHMP).
@graph.author: CFDE / The Human Microbiome Project DCC
@graph.cfde:emptyTablesNote: 27 of the 58 C2M2 tables are empty for this instance and are omitted from @graph; their full schema (fields/PK/FK) is preserved in C2M2_datapackage.json. Populated tables: analysis_type, anatomy, assay_type, biosample, biosample_disease, biosample_from_subject, biosample_gene, biosample_substance, collection_anatomy, collection_compound, collection_disease, collection_protein, collection_substance, collection_taxonomy, compound, data_type, dcc, disease, file_describes_biosample, file_describes_subject, file_format, id_namespace, ncbi_taxonomy, project, project_in_project, protein, subject, subject_disease, subject_role_taxonomy, subject_substance, substance.

@graph.name: C2M2 biosample table schema

id_namespace.description: A CFDE-cleared identifier representing the top-level data space containing this biosample [part 1 of 2-component composite primary key]

local_id.description: An identifier representing this biosample, unique within this id_namespace [part 2 of 2-component composite primary key]

project_id_namespace.description: The id_namespace of the primary project within which this biosample was created [part 1 of 2-component composite foreign key]

project_local_id.description: The local_id of the primary project within which this biosample was created [part 2 of 2-component composite foreign key]

persistent_id.description: A persistent, resolvable (not necessarily retrievable) URI or compact ID permanently attached to this biosample

creation_time.description: An ISO 8601 -- RFC 3339 (subset)-compliant timestamp documenting this biosample's creation time: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS±NN:NN

sample_prep_method.description: An OBI CV term ID (from the 'planned process' branch of the vocabulary, excluding the 'assay' subtree) describing the preparation method that produced this biosample

anatomy.description: An UBERON, CL or CLO CV term ID used to locate the origin of this biosample within the physiology of its source or host organism

biofluid.description: An UBERON CV term or InterLex term used to locate the origin of this biosample within the fluid compartment of its source or host organism

@graph.description: Column schema for the C2M2 'biosample' table, derived from C2M2_datapackage.json. Primary key and foreign keys are preserved in the cfde:* extension fields (fairscape's evi:Schema has no native relational key fields).

@graph.name: C2M2 biosample table (The Human Microbiome Project)
@graph.author: CFDE / The Human Microbiome Project DCC
@graph.description: A tissue sample or other physical specimen
prov:wasAttributedTo: CFDE / The Human Microbiome Project DCC

@graph.name: C2M2 subject table schema

id_namespace.description: A CFDE-cleared identifier representing the top-level data space containing this subject [part 1 of 2-component composite primary key]

local_id.description: An identifier representing this subject, unique within this id_namespace [part 2 of 2-component composite primary key]

project_id_namespace.description: The id_namespace of the primary project within which this subject was studied [part 1 of 2-component composite foreign key]

project_local_id.description: The local_id of the primary project within which this subject was studied [part 2 of 2-component composite foreign key]

persistent_id.description: A persistent, resolvable (not necessarily retrievable) URI or compact ID permanently attached to this subject

creation_time.description: An ISO 8601 -- RFC 3339 (subset)-compliant timestamp documenting this subject record's creation time: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS±NN:NN

granularity.description: A CFDE CV category characterizing this subject by multiplicity

sex.description: A CFDE CV category characterizing the physiological sex of this subject

ethnicity.description: A CFDE CV category characterizing the self-reported ethnicity of this subject

age_at_enrollment.description: The age in years (with a fixed precision of two digits past the decimal point) of this subject when they were first enrolled in the primary project within which they were studied

@graph.description: Column schema for the C2M2 'subject' table, derived from C2M2_datapackage.json. Primary key and foreign keys are preserved in the cfde:* extension fields (fairscape's evi:Schema has no native relational key fields).

@graph.name: C2M2 subject table (The Human Microbiome Project)
@graph.author: CFDE / The Human Microbiome Project DCC
@graph.description: A biological entity from which a C2M2 biosample can in principle be generated
prov:wasAttributedTo: CFDE / The Human Microbiome Project DCC

@graph.name: C2M2 dcc table schema

id.description: The identifier for this DCC, issued by the CFDE-CC

dcc_name.description: A short, human-readable, machine-read-friendly label for this DCC

dcc_abbreviation.description: A very short display label for this contact's DCC

dcc_description.description: A human-readable description of this DCC

contact_email.description: Email address of this DCC's primary technical contact

contact_name.description: Name of this DCC's primary technical contact

dcc_url.description: URL of the front page of the website for this DCC

project_id_namespace.description: ID of the identifier namespace for the project record representing the C2M2 submission produced by this DCC

project_local_id.description: Foreign key identifying the project record representing the C2M2 submission produced by this DCC

@graph.description: Column schema for the C2M2 'dcc' table, derived from C2M2_datapackage.json. Primary key and foreign keys are preserved in the cfde:* extension fields (fairscape's evi:Schema has no native relational key fields).

abbreviation.description: A very short display label for this project

name.description: A short, human-readable, machine-read-friendly label for this project

@graph.name: C2M2 biosample SRS011105
@graph.author: CFDE / The Human Microbiome Project DCC
@graph.description: C2M2 biosample SRS011105 from the The Human Microbiome Project instance.

@graph.name: uterine cervix
@graph.description: Lower, narrow portion of the uterus where it joins with the top end of the vagina.

@graph.name: naris
@graph.description: Orifice of the olfactory system. The naris is the route by which odorants enter the olfactory system.

@graph.name: mouth
@graph.description: The proximal portion of the digestive tract, containing the oral cavity and bounded by the oral opening. In vertebrates, this extends to the pharynx and includes gums, lips, tongue and parts of the palate. Typically also includes the teeth, except where these occur elsewhere (e.g. pharyngeal jaws) or protrude from the mouth (tusks).

@graph.name: blood
@graph.description: A fluid that is composed of blood plasma and erythrocytes.

@graph.name: throat
@graph.description: In anatomy, the throat is the anterior part of the neck, in front of the vertebral column. It consists of the pharynx and larynx. An important feature of the throat is the epiglottis, a flap which separates the esophagus from the trachea and prevents inhalation of food or drink. The throat contains various blood vessels, various pharyngeal muscles, the trachea (windpipe) and the esophagus. The hyoid bone and the clavicle are the only bones located in the throat of mammals. It is sometimes considered a synonym for fauces..

@graph.name: hindlimb stylopod
synonym: proximal segment of free lower limb
@graph.description: The part of the hindlimb between pelvis and the knee, corresponding to the femur.

@graph.name: scalp
@graph.description: The outer covering of the calvaria. It is composed of several layers: SKIN; subcutaneous connective tissue; the occipitofrontal muscle which includes the tendonous galea aponeurotica; Loose connective tissue; and the pericranium (the PERIOSTEUM of the SKULL).

@graph.name: abdomen
@graph.description: The subdivision of the vertebrate body between the thorax and pelvis. The ventral part of the abdomen contains the abdominal cavity and visceral organs. The dorsal part includes the abdominal section of the vertebral column.

@graph.name: stomach
@graph.description: An expanded region of the vertebrate alimentary tract that serves as a food storage compartment and digestive organ. A stomach is lined, in whole or in part by a glandular epithelium.

@graph.name: leg
@graph.description: The portion of the hindlimb that contains both the stylopod and zeugopod.

@graph.name: vagina
@graph.description: A fibromuscular tubular tract leading from the uterus to the exterior of the body in female placental mammals and marsupials, or to the cloaca in female birds, monotremes, and some reptiles.

@graph.name: digestive system
@graph.description: Anatomical system that has as its parts the organs devoted to the ingestion, digestion, and assimilation of food and the discharge of residual wastes.

@graph.name: esophagus
@graph.description: Tube that connects the pharynx to the stomach. In mammals, the oesophagus connects the buccal cavity with the stomach. The stratified squamous non-keratinised epithelium lining the buccal cavity is continued through the pharynx down into the oesophagus. The lowest part of the oesophagus (ca. 2 cm) is lined with gastric mucosa and covered by peritoneum. The main body of the oesophagus is lined with small, simple mucous glands. Each gland opens into the lumen by a long duct which pierces the muscularis mucosae (Wilson and Washington, 1989). A sphincter is situated at the point where the oesophagus enters the stomach to prevent gastro-oesophageal reflux, i.e. to prevent acidic gastric contents from reaching stratified epithelia of the oesophagus, where they can cause inflammation and irritation (Wilson and Washington, 1989; Brown et al., 1993).

@graph.name: rectum
@graph.description: The terminal portion of the intestinal tube, terminating with the anus.

@graph.name: clavicle bone
@graph.description: A paired dermal or endochondral bone that is part of the pectoral girdle. The clavicle may be in contact with the interclavicle or coracoid and forms an attachment site for pectoral musculature..

@graph.name: dorsum
@graph.description: A major subdivision of an organism that is the entire part of the organism dorsal to a horizontal plane and bounded on one side by the same transverse plane. In vertebrares this includes the vertebral column..

@graph.name: caecum
@graph.description: A pouch in the digestive tract that connects the ileum with the ascending colon of the large intestine. It is separated from the ileum by the ileocecal valve, and is the beginning of the large intestine. It is also separated from the colon by the cecocolic junction.

@graph.name: ascending colon
@graph.description: Section of colon which is distal to the cecum and proximal to the transversecolon..

@graph.name: transverse colon
@graph.description: The proximal-distal subdivision of colon that runs transversely across the upper part of the abdomen, from the right to the left colic flexure. Continuous with the descending colon.

@graph.name: descending colon
@graph.description: The portion of the colon between the left colic flexure and the sigmoid colon at the pelvic brim; the portion of the descending colon lying in the left iliac fossa is sometimes called the iliac colon.

@graph.name: sigmoid colon
@graph.description: The part of the large intestine that is closest to the rectum and anus. It forms a loop that averages about 40 cm. in length, and normally lies within the pelvis, but on account of its freedom of movement it is liable to be displaced into the abdominal cavity.

@graph.name: external acoustic meatus
@graph.description: A tube running from the outer ear to the middle ear. The human ear canal extends from the pinna to the eardrum and is about 26 mm in length and 7 mm in diameter.

@graph.name: face
@graph.description: A subdivision of the head that has as parts the layers deep to the surface of the anterior surface, including the mouth, eyes, and nose (when present). In vertebrates, this includes the facial skeleton and structures superficial to the facial skeleton (cheeks, mouth, eyeballs, skin of face, etc).

@graph.name: skin of external ear
synonym: auricular region of head zone of skin
zone of skin of auricular region
zone of skin of auricular region of head
zone of skin of external ear
zone of skin of outer ear
@graph.description: A zone of skin that is part of a external ear.

@graph.name: arm
@graph.description: The part of the forelimb extending from the shoulder to the autopod.

@graph.name: elbow
@graph.description: The elbow is the region surrounding the elbow-joint-the ginglymus or hinge joint in the middle of the arm. Three bones form the elbow joint: the humerus of the upper arm, and the paired radius and ulna of the forearm. The bony prominence at the very tip of the elbow is the olecranon process of the ulna, and the inner aspect of the elbow is called the antecubital fossa..

@graph.name: knee
@graph.description: A segment of the hindlimb that corresponds to the joint connecting a hindlimb stylopod and zeugopod.

@graph.name: shoulder
@graph.description: A subdivision of the pectoral complex consisting of the structures in the region of the shoulder joint (which connects the humerus, scapula and clavicle).

@graph.name: skin of elbow
synonym: zone of skin of cubital region
@graph.description: A zone of skin that is part of a elbow.

@graph.name: lower urinary tract
@graph.description: Subdivision of urinary system which consists of the urinary bladder and the urethra.

@graph.name: nasal cavity
@graph.description: An anatomical cavity that is part of the olfactory apparatus. This includes the space bounded anteriorly by the nares and posteriorly by the choanae, when these structures are present.

@graph.name: nasopharynx
@graph.description: The section of the pharynx that lies above the soft palate.

@graph.name: gingiva
@graph.description: The fibrous investing tissue, covered by keratinized epithelium, that immediately surrounds a tooth and is contiguous with its periodontal ligament and with the mucosal tissues of the mouth.

@graph.name: saliva
@graph.description: A fluid produced in the oral cavity by salivary glands, typically used in predigestion, but also in other functions.

@graph.name: blood plasma
@graph.description: The liquid component of blood, in which erythrocytes are suspended.

@graph.name: blood serum
@graph.description: The portion of blood plasma that excludes clotting factors.

@graph.name: feces
@graph.description: Portion of semisolid bodily waste discharged through the anus.

@graph.name: ileum
@graph.description: The portion of the small intestine that extends from the jejunum to the colon.

@graph.name: palatine tonsil
@graph.description: Tonsils on the left and right sides at the back of the throat. one of the mucosa-associated lymphoid tissues (MALT), located at the entrance to the upper respiratory and gastrointestinal tracts to protect the body from the entry of exogenous material through mucosal sites.

@graph.name: forelimb zeugopod
synonym: intermediate segment of free upper limb
middle limb segment of brachial region
middle limb segment of proximal segment of free upper limb
zeugopod of proximal segment of free upper limb
@graph.description: The middle limb segment of the pectoral free limb, between the autopod and stylopod segments. Includes as parts the forelimb zeugopodial skeleton, which includes as parts the radius and ulna, or their cartilage precursors, or evolutionary variants.

@graph.name: pes
synonym: terminal segment of free lower limb
@graph.description: Distal portion of the hind limb, including tarsal region, metatarsal region and digits.

@graph.name: manus
synonym: terminal segment of free upper limb
@graph.description: Distal segment of the fore limb, including carpal region, metacarpal region and digits.

@graph.name: hard palate
@graph.description: Anterior portion of the palate consisting of bone and mucous membranes. The hard palate is formed from bony processes of the maxilla, premaxilla and palatine.

@graph.name: hindlimb zeugopod
synonym: intermediate segment of free lower limb
middle limb segment of hind limb
@graph.description: The middle limb segment of the pelvic free limb, between the autopod and stylopod segments. Includes as parts the hindlimb zeugopodial skeleton, which includes as parts the tibia and fibula, or their cartilage precursors, or evolutionary variants.

@graph.name: dorsal skin of toe
synonym: skin of dorsal part of toe
subdivision of skin of dorsal part of toe
@graph.description: A dorsal skin of digit that is part of a pedal digit.

@graph.name: occipital region
@graph.description: Anatomical cluster that is located in the posterior region of the cranium and forms the margin of the foramen magnum and occipital condyles.

@graph.name: buccal mucosa
@graph.description: The inner lining of the cheeks and lips.

@graph.name: inguinal part of abdomen
@graph.description: The external junctural region between the lower part of the abdomen and the thigh.

@graph.name: dorsum of tongue
@graph.description: The superior surface of the tongue divided by the sulcus terminalis into an anterior two-thirds, the presulcal part (pars presulcalis). and a posterior one-third, the postsulcal part (pars postsulcalis).

@graph.name: nail of pedal digit
@graph.description: A nail that is part of a pedal digit.

@graph.name: dorsal trunk
@graph.description: The part of the trunk that is in the dorsum.

@graph.name: vagina orifice
@graph.description: The median slit located inferior and posterior to the external urethral orifice in the female; the exit for menstrual flow and birth and the entrance for the penis during sexual intercourse; the size and appearance of the vaginal orifice varies inversely with that of the hymen (G. membrane), a thin fold of mucous membrane that surrounds the vaginal orifice.

@graph.name: popliteal area
@graph.description: A depression on the ventral side of the femur, at the knee joint, between the condyles.

@graph.name: heel
@graph.description: The part of the foot that is the projection of the calcaneus.

@graph.name: subgingival dental plaque
@graph.description: C2M2 anatomy controlled-vocabulary term UBERON:0016484 (Uberon), referenced by the The Human Microbiome Project instance.

@graph.name: supragingival dental plaque
@graph.description: C2M2 anatomy controlled-vocabulary term UBERON:0016485 (Uberon), referenced by the The Human Microbiome Project instance.

@graph.name: posterior fornix of vagina
synonym: posterior part of fornix of vagina
@graph.description: The vaginal fornix that is behind the cervix. It is close to the recto-uterine pouch.

@graph.name: hypothenar eminence
@graph.description: Hypothenar refers to a group of three muscles of the palm that control the motion of the little finger. The three muscles are: Abductor digiti minimi Flexor digiti minimi Opponens digiti minimi.

@graph.name: splenic flexure of colon
@graph.description: The splenic (or left colic) flexure is a sharp bend between the transverse and the descending colon in the left upper quadrant of humans. The left colic flexure is near the spleen, and hence called the splenic flexure. There are two colic flexures in the transverse colon — the other being the hepatic flexure in the right upper quadrant.

@graph.name: hepatic flexure of colon
@graph.description: Hepatic (or the right colic) flexure is the sharp bend between the ascending and the transverse colon. The right colic flexure is adjacent to the liver, and is therefore also known as the hepatic flexure. Thus, the left colic flexure is also known as the splenic flexure (as it is close to the spleen). The hepatic flexure lies in the right upper quadrant of the abdomen in humans.

@graph.name: glabella region
@graph.description: A region of the forehead corresponding to the space between the eyebrows and above the nose.

@graph.name: anterior naris
@graph.description: C2M2 anatomy controlled-vocabulary term UBERON:2001427 (Uberon), referenced by the The Human Microbiome Project instance.

@graph.name: manubrium
@graph.description: C2M2 anatomy controlled-vocabulary term UBERON:2001553 (Uberon), referenced by the The Human Microbiome Project instance.

@graph.name: metabolite profiling assay
@graph.description: An assay that detects and identifies chemical entities resulting from biochemical and cellular metabolism

@graph.name: protein expression profiling assay
@graph.description: An assay that determines protein expression and translation activity using protein extracts collected from a material entity.

@graph.name: RNA-seq assay
synonym: transcription profiling by high throughput sequencing
@graph.description: An RNA sequencing assay that determines an RNA sequence by analyzing the transcibed regions of the genome and or to quantitate transcript abundance.

@graph.name: whole genome sequencing assay
@graph.description: A DNA sequencing assay that intends to provide information about the sequence of an entire genome of an organism.

@graph.name: antigen specific antibodies assay
@graph.description: An analyte assay that measures the presence or amount of antibodies to a specified antigen.

@graph.name: whole metagenome sequencing assay
@graph.description: A DNA sequencing assay that intends to provide information on the DNA sequences of multiple genomes (a metagenome) from different organisms present in the same input sample.

@graph.name: metatranscriptome sequencing assay
@graph.description: An RNA-seq assay that determines the RNA sequence of microbial transcripts from a microbioal community of mixed species from a given microbiome.

@graph.name: lipid profiling assay
@graph.description: An analyte assay assay that detects and identifies lipids resulting from biochemical and cellular metabolism.

@graph.name: 16s ribosomal gene sequencing assay
@graph.description: An amplicon sequencing assay in which the amplicon is derived from universal primers used to amplify the 16S ribosomal RNA gene from isolate bacterial genomic DNA or metagenomic DNA from a microbioal community. Resulting sequences are compared to reference 16S sequence databases to identify or classify bacteria present within a given sample.

@graph.name: cytokine assay
@graph.description: An analyte assay to study presence, concentration, or amount of cytokines.

@graph.name: whole virome sequencing assay
@graph.description: A whole metagenome sequencing assay that intends to provide information on multiple genome sequences from different viruses present in the same input sample.

@graph.name: DamID-seq (provisional term)
@graph.description: DamID-seq is a DamID assay in which samples undergo subsequent high-throughput sequencing, as opposed to microarray or another analysis method.

@graph.name: replicate analysis
@graph.description: A replicate analysis is a data transformation in which data from replicates are combined, e.g. through descriptive statistics calculations, and the results might be utilized for a variety of purposes, like assessing reproducibility, identifying outliers, normalizing, etc.

@graph.name: network analysis
@graph.description: A data transformation that takes as input data that describes biological networks in terms of the node (a.k.a. vertex) and edge graph elements and their characteristics and generates as output properties of the constituent nodes and edges, the sub-graphs, and the entire network.

@graph.name: discriminant analysis
@graph.description: Discriminant function analysis is used to determine which variables discriminate between two or more naturally occurring groups. Analysis is used to determine which variable(s) are the best predictors of a particular outcome.

@graph.name: Protein family report
@graph.description: An informative report on a specific protein family or other classification or group of protein sequences or structures.

@graph.name: Sequence assembly
@graph.description: An assembly of fragments of a (typically genomic) DNA sequence.

@graph.name: Mass spectrum
@graph.description: Spectra from mass spectrometry.

@graph.name: Peptide identification
@graph.description: Protein or peptide identifications with evidence supporting the identifications, for example from comparing a peptide mass fingerprint (from mass spectrometry) to a sequence database, or  the set of typical spectra one obtains when running a protein through a mass spectrometer.

@graph.name: Feature table
@graph.description: Annotation of positional sequence features, organised into a standard feature table.

@graph.name: Alignment
@graph.description: An alignment of molecular sequences, structures or profiles derived from them.

@graph.name: Report
@graph.description: A human-readable collection of information including annotation on a biological entity or phenomena, computer-generated reports of analysis of primary data (e.g. sequence or structural), and metadata (data about primary data) or any other free (essentially unformatted) text, as distinct from the primary data itself.

@graph.name: Matrix
@graph.description: An array of numerical values.

@graph.name: Pathway or network
@graph.description: Primary data about a specific biological pathway or network (the nodes and connections within the pathway or network).

@graph.name: Expression data
@graph.description: Image, hybridisation or some other data arising from a study of feature/molecule expression, typically profiling or quantification.

@graph.name: Protein sequence
@graph.description: One or more protein sequences, possibly with associated annotation.

@graph.name: Taxonomy
@graph.description: Data concerning the classification, identification and naming of organisms.

@graph.name: DNA sequence
@graph.description: A DNA sequence.

@graph.name: RNA sequence
@graph.description: An RNA sequence.

@graph.name: Count matrix
@graph.description: A table of unnormalized values representing summarised read counts per genomic region (e.g. gene, transcript, peak).

@graph.name: Sequence record format
@graph.description: Data format for a molecular sequence record.

@graph.name: FASTA
@graph.description: FASTA format including NCBI-style IDs.

@graph.name: FASTQ
@graph.description: FASTQ short read format ignoring quality scores.

@graph.name: GFF3
@graph.description: Generic Feature Format version 3 (GFF3) of sequence features.

@graph.name: Textual format
@graph.description: Textual format.

@graph.name: BAM
@graph.description: BAM format, the binary, BGZF-formatted compressed version of SAM format for alignment of nucleotide sequences (e.g. sequencing reads) to (a) reference sequence(s). May contain base-call and alignment qualities and other data.

@graph.name: mzML
@graph.description: mzML format for raw spectrometer output data, standardised by HUPO PSI MSS.

@graph.name: JSON
@graph.description: JavaScript Object Notation format; a lightweight, text-based format to represent tree-structured data using key-value pairs.

@graph.name: TSV
@graph.description: Tabular data represented as tab-separated values in a text file.

@graph.name: mzXML
@graph.description: Common file format for proteomics mass spectrometric data developed at the Seattle Proteome Center/Institute for Systems Biology.

@graph.name: Thermo RAW
@graph.description: Proprietary file format for mass spectrometry data from Thermo Scientific.

@graph.name: BIOM format
@graph.description: The BIological Observation Matrix (BIOM) is a format for representing biological sample by observation contingency tables in broad areas of comparative omics. The primary use of this format is to represent OTU tables and metagenome tables.

@graph.name: esophagus adenocarcinoma
@graph.description: An esophageal carcinoma that derives_from epithelial cells of glandular origin.

@graph.name: type 2 diabetes mellitus
@graph.description: A diabetes mellitus that is characterized by high blood sugar, insulin resistance, and relative lack of insulin.

@graph.name: Danaus plexippus
@graph.description: American monarch

@graph.name: Frog virus 3 (isolate Goorha)
@graph.description: C2M2 NCBI taxonomy controlled-vocabulary term NCBI:txid654924 (NCBI Taxonomy), referenced by the The Human Microbiome Project instance.

@graph.name: 001R_FRG3G
@graph.description: Putative transcription factor 001R

@graph.name: 1-chloro-2,4-dinitrobenzene
@graph.description: C2M2 substance controlled-vocabulary term pubchem.substance:1917 (PubChem Substance), referenced by the The Human Microbiome Project instance.

@graph.name: 1-Chloro-2,4-dinitrobenzene
@graph.description: C2M2 compound controlled-vocabulary term pubchem.compound:6 (PubChem Compound), referenced by the The Human Microbiome Project instance.

@graph.name: 9-Ethyladenine
@graph.description: C2M2 compound controlled-vocabulary term pubchem.compound:7 (PubChem Compound), referenced by the The Human Microbiome Project instance.

@graph.name: G00025YC
@graph.description: type=Saccharide mass=1989.7294541 permass=2466.26155647 monosaccharides=11 is_motif=False missing_score=115

@graph.name: C2M2_datapackage.json
@graph.author: CFDE / The Human Microbiome Project DCC
@graph.description: The Frictionless Data Package descriptor (JSON Table Schema) defining all 58 C2M2 table fields, primary keys, and foreign-key relationships for this instance — the authoritative relational contract, preserved verbatim so the full model survives even for tables omitted from @graph.
prov:wasAttributedTo: CFDE / The Human Microbiome Project DCC

@graph.name: C2M2 to RO-Crate conversion (The Human Microbiome Project)
@graph.description: Conversion of the The Human Microbiome Project C2M2 Frictionless datapackage into this RO-Crate: one Dataset + evi:Schema per populated table, the file table additionally exploded into file-level source-data entities, and the datapackage.json + SQLite preserved verbatim.
prov:wasAssociatedWith: CFDE / The Human Microbiome Project DCC
@graph.runBy: CFDE / The Human Microbiome Project DCC

@graph.name: CFDE C2M2 to RO-Crate Converter
@graph.author: CFDE / FAIRSCAPE
@graph.description: Reference converter that reads a C2M2 Frictionless datapackage (datapackage.json + TSVs + SQLite) and emits a FAIRSCAPE RO-Crate following the C2M2-to-ROCrate design spec.
prov:wasAttributedTo: CFDE / FAIRSCAPE
